# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |
|-------|-------|--------|------------------|-----------|
| #722 | bug(replan): after a repair-replan commits, the child epoch's first gate close refuses review_journal_plan_hash_mismatch — the cross-epoch schema-2 journal rotation is missing | ready — unblocked (#725 closed, v6.24.0 shipped 2026-07-21) | — | BATCH 5 (cross-epoch journal; absorbed #738) — committed-import resolution in the schema-2 reader + V2 child-bound journal on first settlement + the two sequential-gate reader seams + sequential-two-gate child-epoch test; after BATCH 4 (end-to-end replan tests need prepare working) |
| #727 | enhancement(adaptive/node): gate evidence seed carries no domain_outcome allowed-values hint — a wrong token (pass) only surfaces at close as review_domain_outcome_invalid | ready — audited+confirmed 2026-07-19 | — | BATCH 6 — moved from papercuts: edits the same evidence-seed generator + close-time review-evidence validation block as #728; domain_outcome enum hint |
| #728 | bug(review-v2): change-gate adversarial refutation can settle with zero canonical findings and poison convergence | ready — first implementation REJECTED 2026-07-21 (bound at the wrong seam, false-blocked a legal fanout); root cause CONFIRMED and unchanged | — | ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: parseReviewEvidence harvests only finding_json:/resolution_json: rows, so a schema-1 flat `finding:` row yields findings:[]; the coherence pair in validateSchema2ReviewEvidence is gated off for adversarial-verifier; deriveGateEffect still returns fail; assessReviewProgress then sees [] vs [] -> review_frontier_nonprogress -> review_nonconvergent + replan_required. REJECTION: the guard was keyed on the MEMBER's own deriveGateEffect==='fail', but for a fanout the SETTLED attempt is the REDUCED aggregate — reduceReviewReceipts absorbs a minority dissent, so a legal replicated_majority gate with one abstaining (indeterminate) replica was hard-refused and could not complete, and the only escape was to FABRICATE a failure_class + trigger digests for a defect the reviewer explicitly could not determine. The implementer proved SUFFICIENCY (every aggregate fail has >=1 member fail) and treated it as NECESSITY. REDO: bind the invariant at the AGGREGATE/settlement seam — a failed/refuted SETTLED attempt must never carry an empty canonical frontier — and pin it with a replicated_majority fixture (no existing test covers an AV fanout under this rule). |
| #729 | bug(replan): empty or uncovered failed-review findings can activate a review-only child epoch | ready — unblocked (#725 closed, v6.24.0 shipped 2026-07-21) — audited+confirmed 2026-07-19 | — | BATCH 7 (carry-forward, with #730) — defense-in-depth once #728 lands: no review-only child epoch from empty/uncovered findings |
| #730 | bug(repair-dispatch): reopened fixer receives IDs but not a digest-bound canonical finding brief | ready — unblocked (#725 closed, v6.24.0 shipped 2026-07-21) — audited 2026-07-19 (severity reframed, scope trimmed; see body revision) | — | BATCH 7 — digest-bound canonical finding brief for reopened fixers; the brief contract also seeds descendant replays |
| #731 | bug(adversarial profile): discovery can stop at first refutation and closure repeats broad search | ready — unblocked (#725 closed, v6.24.0 shipped 2026-07-21) — audited+confirmed 2026-07-19 (#728 is a hard prerequisite) | — | BATCH 6 — after #728 in-batch; adversarial profile discovery-vs-closure behavior (tandem SECTION_IDS + REQUIRED_BEHAVIOR_TOKENS edits) |
| #732 | bug(replan): prepare loses the committed predecessor after sanctioned scratch reset and emits predecessor:null for epoch 3+ | ready — unblocked (#725 closed, v6.24.0 shipped 2026-07-21) — consumer-run defect (financial-agent #332, edition 4.23.1) | — | BATCH 8 (epoch lineage bookkeeping, with #724) — keep the committed predecessor across a sanctioned scratch reset (epoch 3+) |
| #733 | bug(adaptive): settled failed schema-2 review rewinds the plan ledger but leaves workflow-tasks.json in_progress | ready — unblocked (#725 closed, v6.24.0 shipped 2026-07-21) — consumer-run defect (financial-agent #332) | — | BATCH 3 rider — task-mirror regeneration atomicity after a review rewind (same replan.js region as the authority checks) |
| #735 | bug(claim): discard/release structurally refuse state_compliance_authority_invalid on schema-2 adaptive projects (frozen-never-run AND mid-run windows) | ready — unblocked (#725 closed, v6.24.0 shipped 2026-07-21) | — | BATCH 3 — rides the #719 direction; additionally discard/release must not demand run-state artifacts a never-run frozen project has not produced |
| #743 | chore(adaptive): retire the escalated_to_full halt-marker vestige — the full path it references no longer exists | PARTIALLY SHIPPED — the dual-marker retirement landed (0308f78f, merged 35e4800d); the naming half is deliberately unimplemented | — | SHIPPED: write-halt no longer co-writes the vestigial escalated_to_full:security half beside consent; all four halt reasons share one marker-splice path (net -123 lines). Proven dead first (zero security_halt hits repo-wide; every live reader takes the FIRST marker line). clear-halt stays a superset and still strips the legacy half, pinned by T-360d, because the four installed v6.24.0 runtimes can still write the dual marker. REMAINING (value call, not deferred work): retiring the escalated_to_full NAME. The key is read by repair-state, orient and per-edition codex-compact-resume and appears in in-flight workflow-state.md, so a rename needs a dual-name tolerance path — ADDING a compatibility mechanism to buy a cosmetic rename, which is the wrong direction against the reduce-redundancy objective. Re-scope to the naming question with that cost stated, or close as satisfied-in-substance. |
| #744 | audit(adaptive): judgment-call redundancy register from the 2026-07-21 post-pruning sweep — decide keep/prune per item | needs-decision — maintainer value calls required before any code moves | — | UNBATCHED decision register — close by deciding keep/prune per item; surviving prunes join a future papercut batch |
| #745 | bug(plan-validator): interior code-reviewer/security-reviewer walls still false-block on disjoint downstream writes — the surface-freshness rescue covers only adversarial-verifier, and their reopen remedy still dead-ends | PARTIALLY SHIPPED — the false-block half landed (merge window ending 7d0df364); the reopen-remedy half is deliberately unimplemented | — | SHIPPED: interiorSurfaceFresh widened to code-reviewer/security-reviewer, with the producer slice RECOMPUTED from the frozen graph (not trusted from the journal) and a final certifier counting as a live whole-candidate wall only when its ledger row is actually complete. REMAINING: the reopen remedy. Auto-parking a "provably pristine" sink was REJECTED as unsafe, not deferred — the sink runs main-session-direct and its first irreversible step writes no in-band marker, so a pristine sink cannot be distinguished from one that already committed/pushed/merged. A safe remedy needs an explicit sink-progress marker. Cross-epoch freshness (seal attempt absent after journal rotation) also still falls back to whole-candidate. |
| #753 | bug(plan-validator): --finalize-check attribution sweep ignores the epoch lineage — the other half of the multi-epoch finalize block | ready — filed 2026-07-21 from adversarial verification of the #724 barrier fix; confirmed by code read, not yet reproduced end-to-end | — | Mirror the #724 barrier fix into the finalize attribution sweep: completeDeclared is built from parseNodes(content) — the CHILD plan only — and refuses unattributed_change on parent-epoch paths, so a replanned run now clears the barrier and is refused at finalize on the SAME paths. Reuse verifyAllEpochSnapshots (do not write a second lineage walker), select epochs by INTEGER INDEX (parent_plan_hash is not a unique byte pin), fail closed with a typed lineage reason. CRITICAL: extend the attribution floor the same way the barrier's was — fold parent nodes keyed to THEIR OWN epoch ledger status, or the union becomes a laundering primitive. Blocked in practice on #754 (the archived repro artifact fails snapshot verification for the unrelated mode-drift reason), so either fix #754 first or build a synthetic fixture with genuine seals. |
| #754 | bug(replan): snapshot manifest compares exact file mode, so any git round-trip permanently breaks epoch verification | ready — filed 2026-07-21; PRE-EXISTING, measured on the archived multi-epoch artifact and independently reproduced twice | — | verifySnapshotManifest compares the recorded mode against stat.mode & 0o777 while replan writes some files 0600 and git does not preserve it. Measured: epoch 1 has 1 mismatch, epoch 2 has 3, all want=600 got=644, EVERY content digest matching. Compare only bits that survive git (realistically the executable bit) or drop the mode comparison and state that snapshot integrity is content-addressed. Do NOT weaken the content digest — that is the load-bearing check. Higher impact since #724 added a fourth caller of the walk. Unblocks the #753 reproduction fixture. |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
